description: Draft governance-toolkit-only corpus after removing AMP, BadGuys, and PatchHub authority content.
Machine-readable authority binding model for resolver and constraint-pack generation.

authoritative_semantics: The issue-specific authority pack MUST contain target scope, spec fingerprint, active bindings, active rule ids, full rule text, match basis, authoritative sources, shared contracts, downstream consumers, exception refs, required wiring, forbidden strategies, required validation, verification mode, verification method, oracle refs, and aggregate scope metadata. hash_pack.txt MUST be exactly one lowercase ASCII hex SHA-256 line over raw constraint_pack.json bytes.

Discovery resolver MUST fail closed on ambiguous symbol, duplicate semantics without allowed dedup, conflicting obligations, missing binding for authority-driven scope, missing verification contract, and pack recompute mismatch.
Final resolver MUST recompute the active authority pack from the authoritative corpus and MUST STOP on any mismatch, ambiguity, conflicting obligation set, or missing verification contract.

description: Constraint pack oracle. The implementation bridge artifact MUST carry a recomputable pack and a one-line lowercase SHA-256 hash over raw constraint_pack.json bytes.
Discovery resolver oracle. Ambiguous symbol, duplicate semantics, conflicting obligations, missing binding, and missing verification contract MUST stop the workflow.
Final resolver oracle. Pack recompute mismatch, ambiguity, conflicting obligations, and missing verification contract MUST stop the workflow.

statement: Workflow-aware governance toolkit behavior MUST be specified in governance/specification.jsonl; governance/governance.jsonl remains the authoritative source of governance workflow data consumed by the toolkit.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-aware toolkit behavior

statement: Workflow-aware toolkit components MUST derive next-step, gate, invalidation, rollback, and required_substeps guidance from authoritative governance workflow data and MUST NOT invent shadow workflow maps.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-aware toolkit behavior

statement: If a workflow-aware toolkit component cannot derive required workflow guidance from authoritative governance data, it MUST HARD STOP rather than infer missing workflow structure.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-aware toolkit behavior

statement: For pm_validator.py, the external-gate surface MUST be limited to pytest, ruff, mypy, typescript, and biome.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For a pm_validator.py external gate, the default execution model MUST be: untriggered gate -> SKIP; triggered gate with successful execution and exit code 0 -> PASS; triggered gate with successful execution and non-zero exit code -> FAIL; triggered gate that could not be executed -> UNVERIFIED_ENVIRONMENT.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py external gates, a reported UNVERIFIED_ENVIRONMENT outcome MUST be treated as an execution outcome of that external gate and MUST NOT, by itself, be classified as a hard-rule blocked verification state for the overall PM validator result unless authority explicitly states otherwise.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py external gates, the overall PM validator result MUST become FAIL when a triggered external gate is actually executed and returns FAIL; a reported external-gate UNVERIFIED_ENVIRONMENT outcome alone MUST NOT automatically force overall FAIL; existing hard-rule FAIL semantics for other validator rules remain unchanged.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: pm_validator.py MUST accept an optional CLI flag --skip-external-gates.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: When --skip-external-gates is active, pm_validator.py MUST NOT execute external gates and MUST emit SKIP for each skipped external gate with detail cli_disabled.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: The --skip-external-gates flag MUST apply only to external gates and MUST NOT disable instructions/pack integrity and recompute checks, target checks, docs gate, git apply --check, Python compile, JS syntax, or monolith checks.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py baseline invocation, INSTRUCTIONS_ZIP remains a required positional argument. pm_validator.py MUST aggregate verdicts across the full covered baseline validation flow and MUST NOT stop immediately after a non-prerequisite FAIL. If the instructions artifact is missing, unreadable, or the pack layer fails, pm_validator.py MUST emit explicit instructions/pack FAIL verdicts and MUST continue into every remaining deterministically runnable covered check.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: When a later pm_validator.py check cannot run because an earlier prerequisite did not produce the required materialized input, pm_validator.py MUST emit an explicit verdict for that check with status SKIP and detail prerequisite_unsatisfied instead of terminating the run silently.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: pm_validator.py MUST emit the final RESULT line only after every covered check in the current invocation has produced an explicit verdict or an explicit prerequisite_unsatisfied SKIP verdict. This aggregation rule MUST NOT weaken existing overall FAIL semantics for hard-rule FAIL outcomes.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py patch-member collection, member-local checks that do not depend on a successful previous member verdict MUST be evaluated across the full member set and MUST emit all detectable FAIL verdicts before the validator decides whether apply-dependent stages are runnable.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For implementation_scope/final controller context, workflow-aware governance toolkit components MUST compute an effective prestart context consisting of the selected entry workflow step plus all recursively required_substeps derived from authoritative governance workflow data.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: The effective prestart step order MUST be deterministic: entry step first, then recursive required_substeps in listed order, with first-seen-wins deduplication; from that order, toolkit components MUST derive effective capabilities in step order with first-seen-wins deduplication.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: From the effective capabilities order, workflow-aware governance toolkit components MUST derive effective rule ids and effective full rule text in the same order; if a referenced rule object is missing, the toolkit MUST HARD STOP.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: rc_resolver.py MUST emit workflow_effective_step_ids, workflow_effective_capabilities, workflow_effective_rule_ids, and workflow_effective_full_rule_text in constraint_pack.json without changing the meaning of existing active_bindings or full_rule_text fields; pm_validator_pack_contract.py MUST recompute and verify the same fields using the same traversal semantics.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: gov_navigator.py MUST render authority-derived full rule text taken from authoritative rule.statement values, not only rule ids, in every navigator view whose content presents rule-bearing constraints either directly through rule references or indirectly through capabilities that trigger rules. At minimum this includes ENTRY SURFACES, ROUTE NAVIGATION, CAPABILITY NAVIGATION, IMPLEMENTATION NAVIGATION, ENTRY GATES, WORKFLOW ROOTS, WORKFLOW STEP DETAILS, and EFFECTIVE PRESTART RULES.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: For implementation_scope/final controller entry, authoritative governance workflow data MUST expose a dedicated workflow root that carries prestart context via required_substeps; toolkit components MUST consume that root from governance/governance.jsonl and MUST NOT synthesize a shadow prestart map, and workflow_effective_context.py MUST be the single traversal authority reused by rc_resolver.py, gov_navigator.py, and pm_validator_pack_contract.py. In gov_navigator.py, rule ids MAY appear only as secondary references and MUST NOT be the sole normative content where authority-derived full rule text is required, and any JSON output that already exposes workflow root or step constraint data MUST carry the same authority-derived full rule text in the same deterministic order as the paired text view.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Workflow-effective prestart context

statement: This pm_validator.py external-gate contract MUST NOT reinterpret governance/governance.jsonl. Hard-rule blocked verification state semantics in governance/governance.jsonl remain unchanged for rules that authority already defines as hard-rule blocked verification state.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py initial mode with an authoritative workspace snapshot, the validator MUST materialize the full workspace snapshot tree into the temp validation root before apply-dependent checks, compile checks, JS syntax checks, monolith checks, or external gates run. Changed-file selection remains the scope of validation, but the execution context MUST be the full snapshot tree, not a decision-path-only subset.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py repair mode without --workspace-snapshot, the validator MUST enter explicit repair-overlay-only mode. In repair-overlay-only mode, the authoritative filesystem baseline MUST consist only of repo-relative files physically present in the authoritative --repair-overlay artifact, and patch decision paths MUST be partitioned deterministically into overlay-covered paths and overlay-uncovered paths.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: In pm_validator.py repair-overlay-only mode, the validator MUST always execute all patch-archive checks that do not require workspace snapshot contents, including patch basename checks, COMMIT_MESSAGE.txt checks, ISSUE_NUMBER.txt checks, target.txt presence and format checks, repair overlay target extraction and equality checks, per-file patch layout checks, patch-member path and header checks, ASCII checks, line-length checks, and docs gate checks. File-content and temp-tree checks, including git apply --check, patch apply, Python compile checks, JavaScript syntax checks, monolith checks, and external gates, MUST run only for overlay-covered paths. For every overlay-uncovered path, the validator MUST emit an explicit degraded verdict naming that exact repo-relative path and MUST NOT abort the repair run solely because --workspace-snapshot is absent. In repair mode, the only snapshot-dependent checks are workspace-snapshot basename to overlay-target consistency and supplemental-authority file sourcing outside the overlay; if --workspace-snapshot is absent, those checks MUST emit explicit degraded verdicts instead of hard abort.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: pm_validator.py, rc_resolver.py, and pm_validator_pack_contract.py MUST support a degraded validator mode for a repository snapshot or authoritative repair overlay that does not contain governance/specification.jsonl. In that mode, repo-spec-dependent resolver and pack-recompute branches MUST emit explicit degraded verdicts instead of hard abort or shadow-spec synthesis, while every remaining validator check that does not depend on governance/specification.jsonl MUST continue to run.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: For pm_validator.py initial full-snapshot mode, repair-overlay-only mode, and repository-specification-absent degraded mode, the covered runnable validation flow MUST include every deterministically runnable check authorized by the available inputs for that invocation. pm_validator.py MUST aggregate verdicts across that full covered runnable flow and MUST NOT fail-fast after a non-prerequisite FAIL or degraded resolver/pack outcome; when a later check is not runnable because a required materialized input is absent, the validator MUST emit an explicit verdict for that check instead of terminating the run silently.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: Governance toolkit behavior MUST support a bootstrap-first authority workspace input obtained from an authorized remote repository source; for that mode, the authority workspace input is the full repository tree.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Toolkit authority workspace transport and portability

statement: In bootstrap-first mode, the governance toolkit MUST use the full repository tree as the authority workspace execution context and MUST NOT redefine that mode as a partial file-subset contract.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Toolkit authority workspace transport and portability

statement: An uploaded workspace snapshot zip remains a supported fallback transport for the same authority workspace content, but it MUST NOT be the only permitted input transport for governance toolkit operation.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Toolkit authority workspace transport and portability

statement: If the governance toolkit already possesses the full authoritative repository content in a deterministic repo-relative content model, it MUST NOT require creation of an intermediate workspace snapshot zip solely for its own internal processing.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Toolkit authority workspace transport and portability

statement: Governance toolkit operation MUST NOT depend on the toolkit's own home directory, concrete bundle layout, or current working directory relative to the toolkit itself; relocating the toolkit bundle MUST NOT by itself change the toolkit's ability to process the same authority workspace input.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Toolkit authority workspace transport and portability

statement: Bootstrap fetch of the full repository tree and fallback uploaded snapshot zip are alternative transport mechanisms for the same authority workspace content; canonical authority source paths and authority semantics remain unchanged, and toolkit behavior MUST NOT create a second truth between those transport modes.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Toolkit authority workspace transport and portability

statement: When a governance toolkit check requires a materialized filesystem tree, the toolkit MAY create a temporary working tree from the authoritative full-repository content model, but that materialization is only an execution substrate and MUST NOT introduce a new authority layer or a new mandatory artifact contract.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > Toolkit authority workspace transport and portability

statement: For pm_validator.py, EXTERNAL_GATE:PYTEST MUST be triggered whenever changed decision paths contain at least one existing repo-relative file with suffix .py or .js in the materialized validation tree; when no such changed file exists, EXTERNAL_GATE:PYTEST MUST emit SKIP with detail not_triggered.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: When EXTERNAL_GATE:PYTEST is triggered, pm_validator.py MUST execute pytest against the materialized full repository tree as the execution context and MUST NOT narrow the pytest invocation to selected changed test-file paths.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: The pytest-specific trigger and execution-scope contract MUST reuse the existing external-gate outcome semantics: a triggered EXTERNAL_GATE:PYTEST that executes and returns non-zero MUST yield FAIL and force the overall PM validator result to FAIL, while a pytest UNVERIFIED_ENVIRONMENT outcome alone MUST NOT automatically force overall FAIL.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates

statement: This pytest-specific authority change MUST NOT alter the trigger semantics, changed-file selection scope, or command-scoping contract of EXTERNAL_GATE:RUFF, EXTERNAL_GATE:MYPY, EXTERNAL_GATE:TYPESCRIPT, or EXTERNAL_GATE:BIOME unless authority explicitly changes those gates in governance/specification.jsonl.
heading_path: Governance toolkit specification > PM validator external gates
